title: Georges Clairin

infobox.value: [Isidore Pils](/wiki/Isidore_Pils "Isidore Pils"); [François-Édouard Picot](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois-%C3%89douard_Picot "François-Édouard Picot"); [École des beaux-arts de Paris](/wiki/%C3%89cole_nationale_sup%C3%A9rieure_des_Beaux-Arts "École nationale supérieure des Beaux-Arts")

(11 September 1843, Paris – Pouldu, [Clohars-Carnoët](/wiki/Clohars-Carno%C3%ABt "Clohars-Carnoët") 2 September 1919) was a French Orientalist painter and illustrator. He was influenced by Eastern imagery [Moorish](/wiki/Moors "Moors") architecture, and visited North Africa many times, in particular Algeria, Morocco and Egypt. In Paris he led the life of a socialite, and befriended the glamorous actress [Sarah Bernhardt](/wiki/Sarah_Bernhardt "Sarah Bernhardt"), his friend for 50 years, and is today best known for his 'in costume' and informal intimate portraits of her.[[1]](#cite_note-r-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French painter and illustrator (1843–1919)

| Georges Clairin | |
| --- | --- |
| Georges Clairin depicted by [Paul Mathey](/wiki/Paul_Mathey "Paul Mathey") | |
| Born | (1843-09-11)11 September 1843  [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [France](/wiki/France "France") |
| Died | 2 September 1919(1919-09-02) (aged 75)  [Clohars-Carnoët](/wiki/Clohars-Carno%C3%ABt "Clohars-Carnoët"), France |
| Education | [Isidore Pils](/wiki/Isidore_Pils "Isidore Pils"); [François-Édouard Picot](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois-%C3%89douard_Picot "François-Édouard Picot"); [École des beaux-arts de Paris](/wiki/%C3%89cole_nationale_sup%C3%A9rieure_des_Beaux-Arts "École nationale supérieure des Beaux-Arts") |
| Movement | [Orientalist](/wiki/Orientalism "Orientalism") |

**Georges Jules Victor Clairin** (11 September 1843, Paris – Pouldu, [Clohars-Carnoët](/wiki/Clohars-Carno%C3%ABt "Clohars-Carnoët") 2 September 1919) was a French Orientalist painter and illustrator. He was influenced by Eastern imagery [Moorish](/wiki/Moors "Moors") architecture, and visited North Africa many times, in particular Algeria, Morocco and Egypt. In Paris he led the life of a socialite, and befriended the glamorous actress [Sarah Bernhardt](/wiki/Sarah_Bernhardt "Sarah Bernhardt"), his friend for 50 years, and is today best known for his 'in costume' and informal intimate portraits of her.[[1]](#cite_note-r-1)

## Life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Georges_Clairin&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dd/Clairin%2C_Georges%2C_BNF_Gallica.jpg/250px-Clairin%2C_Georges%2C_BNF_Gallica.jpg)](/wiki/File:Clairin,_Georges,_BNF_Gallica.jpg)

Georges Clairin

Clairin was apprenticed in the workshops of [Isidore Pils](/wiki/Isidore_Pils "Isidore Pils") and [François-Édouard Picot](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois-%C3%89douard_Picot "François-Édouard Picot"). In 1861 he entered the [École des beaux-arts de Paris](/wiki/%C3%89cole_nationale_sup%C3%A9rieure_des_Beaux-Arts "École nationale supérieure des Beaux-Arts"),[[1]](#cite_note-r-1) and in 1866 first displayed his work. He travelled to Spain with [Henri Regnault](/wiki/Henri_Regnault "Henri Regnault") and to Italy with [François Flameng](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Flameng "François Flameng") and [Jean-Léon Gérôme](/wiki/Jean-L%C3%A9on_G%C3%A9r%C3%B4me "Jean-Léon Gérôme"). He met the Catalan painter [Marià Fortuny](/wiki/Mari%C3%A0_Fortuny "Marià Fortuny") in Morocco and they visited [Tétouan](/wiki/T%C3%A9touan "Tétouan") together.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1895, he travelled to Egypt with the composer [Camille Saint-Saëns](/wiki/Camille_Saint-Sa%C3%ABns "Camille Saint-Saëns").

He is best known for his portraits of [Sarah Bernhardt](/wiki/Sarah_Bernhardt "Sarah Bernhardt"), with whom he had a long friendship and whom he depicted in costume for a number of her roles, including as the queen in *[Ruy Blas](/wiki/Ruy_Blas "Ruy Blas")* (1879), Mélisande in *[La Princesse Lointaine](/wiki/La_Princesse_Lointaine "La Princesse Lointaine")* (1895 and 1899), Cleopatra (1900), Theodora (1902) and Saint Teresa of Ávila; he also showed her in less formal poses. Clairin painted many ceilings, among them the foyers of the [Opéra Garnier](/wiki/Op%C3%A9ra_Garnier "Opéra Garnier") (1874) and the Le Trident, the theatre of Cherbourg.

He was the uncle of the painter [Pierre-Eugène Clairin](/w/index.php?title=Pierre-Eug%C3%A8ne_Clairin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pierre-Eugène Clairin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pierre-Eug%C3%A8ne_Clairin "fr:Pierre-Eugène Clairin")].

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Sarah_Bernhardt_Clairin_Henderson.jpg/250px-Sarah_Bernhardt_Clairin_Henderson.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sarah_Bernhardt_Clairin_Henderson.jpg)

*Portrait de Sarah Bernhardt* (1876), Musée du [Petit Palais](/wiki/Petit_Palais "Petit Palais")

## Portrait of Sarah Bernhardt, 1876

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Georges_Clairin&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Portrait of Sarah Bernhardt, 1876")]

Clairin's 1876 portrait of Sarah Bernhardt drew praise. Théodore Véron said of it:

> The portrait of Mademoiselle Sarah Bernhardt is assuredly one of the most prominent of the Salon, as much for the originality of its composition as for the splendour of its colour.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-salon-4)

> Monsieur Clarin shows us her wrapped in a long robe of white satin with a trailing train, she is resting on a luxurious divan of pink satin and leaning on a cushion of the same material draped in gold; to her right and in the depths of this Oriental apartment is a Venetian mirror surrounded by purple velvet curtains; to her left a tropical plant lowers its green leaves over the meditating actress and sculptress; at here feet a yellow hound of a large breed rests on its long legs and stretches out its proud and aristocratic head.[[4]](#cite_note-salon-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[Emile Zola](/wiki/Emile_Zola "Emile Zola") found that: "Mademoiselle Sarah Bernhardt isn't pretty but she has fine intelligent features and Clairin has been able to give her a smooth little face and vulgar sensuality like [Cabanel](/wiki/Cabanel "Cabanel") would paint."[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Georges_Clairin&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

In the 2024 French biopic *[Sarah Bernhardt, la Divine](/w/index.php?title=Sarah_Bernhardt,_la_Divine&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sarah Bernhardt, la Divine (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarah_Bernhardt,_la_Divine "fr:Sarah Bernhardt, la Divine")]*, Clairin was played by [Clément Hervieu-Léger](/w/index.php?title=Cl%C3%A9ment_Hervieu-L%C3%A9ger&action=edit&redlink=1 "Clément Hervieu-Léger (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cl%C3%A9ment_Hervieu-L%C3%A9ger "fr:Clément Hervieu-Léger")] of the [Comédie Française](/wiki/Com%C3%A9die_Fran%C3%A7aise "Comédie Française").

2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Lynne Thornton (1985), www.acr-edition.com (ed.), *La Femme dans la peinture orientaliste*, www.acr-edition.com, p. 264, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [2867700116](/wiki/Special:BookSources/2867700116 "Special:BookSources/2867700116"), p. 232.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** Le portrait de Mme Sara Bernhardt est assurément une des œuvres les plus saillantes du Salon, tant par l'originalité de la composition que par la splendeur du coloris
4. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-salon_4-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-salon_4-1) Théodore Véron (1876). [*Le salon de 1876 : mémorial de l'art et des artistes de mon temps*](http://catalogue.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/cb31564848s/description). Poitiers.`{{cite book}}`: CS1 maint: location missing publisher ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_location_missing_publisher "Category:CS1 maint: location missing publisher"))
5. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** M. Clairin nous la représente enveloppée dans un long peignoir de satin blanc à queue traînante, elle est étendue sur un riche divan de satin rosé, et accoudée sur un coussin de même étoffe chamarrée d'or; à sa droite et dans le fond de cet appartement tout oriental, est une glace de Venise entourée de rideaux de velours violet; à sa gauche une plante tropicale abaisse ses larges feuilles vertes sur l'actrice et sculpteur en méditation; à ses pieds, un lévrier jaune de grande race repose sur ses longues pattes et allonge sa tête fière et aristocratique
6. **[^](#cite_ref-6)** Mlle Sarah Bernhard n'est pas jolie, mais elle a des traits fins et intelligents dont Clairin n'a su faire qu'un minois régulier et vulgairement sensuel tel que le peindrait un Cabanel "( Salon de 1876)
